video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Nashville with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Cafe Nona
restaurants.guy_intro: So about two miles west of downtown Nashville on Murphy Road to check out a joint where a chef who's a transplant from New York City had a couple places. You've probably heard of him. The Dancing Bear, the Red Rooster, and for the last 15 years, he's been rocking this place. This is Cafe Nona. The home-style preparation feels like you're sitting at Grandma's house. So it's fitting Chef Danny Mangiapito named the joint for the woman who first got him into cooking.

dishes.description: Eight layers high with whole milk ricotta, cream, shallots, garlic, sage, oregano, basil, red pepper, salt, pepper, marinara, fresh lasagna sheets, spinach, butternut squash, Parmesan, mozzarella. Baked, then topped with Mornay sauce, steamed Swiss chard, and marinara. Served with fried julienne carrots, zucchini, and squash.
dishes.guy_response: And this is the kind of lasagna that your grandmother would make when you were a kid. This is sort of my version. I wanted to put a little spin on it. You know what I like is look at the amount of spinach this guy puts in his lasagna. Nice big thick noodles in there. You definitely don't hold back on the cheese. Three different sauces in there. Okay. I mean you put a lot of time work into it. Yes, it tastes like a nice job. Thank you

dishes.description: Leg of lamb cooked in spices including cardamom, cinnamon, cumin, turmeric, saffron, garlic, and ginger. Sauce made with spices, tomato puree, sour cream, homemade yogurt, and milk, cooked for 3.5 hours. Assembled with spice water, dry cherry, raisins, and cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Hmm. Are you kidding me? Fantastic you are surrounded in flavor Wow You probably not have lamb this tender. Hmm. This is like Mediterranean bolognese. I'm sorry. I got to do it one more bite This is bottle cap. She's dangerous trained ninja in flavor

dishes.description: A huge sandwich with burger patties, American cheese, bacon, a chicken tender (buttermilk marinated, corn flake breaded, fried), onion rings, mozzarella stick, jalapeno popper, and snappy sauce. Served with lettuce, tomato, house-made bread and butter pickles, and a pepperoncini.
dishes.guy_response: Right, the chicken tender was perfectly cooked nice and moist great breading on the outside of it From the hot burger and the melting cheese to the cold tomato cold lettuce. There was just a nice little juxtaposition there There's two things that really stand out one is a snappy sauce Dynamite thank you The pepper do is a kiss of acidity and sweetness to the bread butter pickles that you guys make are awesome served up with dynamite Fries you order burger like that? You'll be telling all your friends about bottle cap Titan sandwich for you The chicken is just really really scrumptious you get the spice from the jalapeno popper on top

restaurants.name: Eastside Banh Mi
restaurants.guy_intro: Here to check out a joint where a husband-and-wife team left San Francisco where they had this really successful fast casual Vietnamese concept and they came out here to offer up the folks in Nashville some real banh mi. This is Eastside banh mi.

dishes.description: A cold cut banh mi featuring house-made ham hock terrine, pate, and butter mayo. Built on a three-day process baguette with cucumbers, bologna, pickled daikon radish, carrots, cilantro, jalapenos, and seasoning sauce.
dishes.guy_response: That's never level right there. That's goosebumps The pate is lights out so rich velvety silky The bread is a crunch of fire getting great texture out of that local bologna. Definitely texture out of that terrain Silkiness and creaminess and fat coming through from that buttered Mayo a little bit of spice from the jalapeno palette refresher from the pickled onions and daikon Actions legendary probably some of the best spot me I've had number one bun me table seven the ham hocks arena the pate adds like a nice smoky aspect then you balance that with the Cilantro and the cucumber it makes really kind of sing in the mouth really what brought me in the most was the bread Right, you're dead kills. Yeah, you have the shallot mayonnaise. I want to put it on everything. It's It's phenomenal. It's different than any sandwich. I've ever had

restaurants.name: Soy Bistro
restaurants.guy_intro: This is soy bistro. I go to the patio. We have to go to Jane chicken big fan of the place Can't come here enough traditional with some chicken, baby delicious Asian soul food.

dishes.description: Chicken brined in water, salt, sugar, garlic, peppercorn, lemon, and bay leaves. Served with a chili oil made from vegetable oil, garlic, Korean chili flakes, onion powder, salt, and cayenne. Accompanied by a cream wasabi coleslaw and topped with sriracha, chili oil, butter, toasted sesame seeds, and green onions.
dishes.guy_response: Delicious. All right. There's a little bit of heat but not overwhelmingly hot like you're burning your mouth and it's just all cayenne loaded tender chicken perfect rice nice little slaw to cool you down if it's too hot It's a nice alternative because it's not fried So you're getting the the fix that you want without all the calories that you probably don't need Delicious very approachable of the hot chicken It has a little bit of a spice to it But it tastes great, especially with some of the dressings that come with it making the right thing

restaurants.name: Pie Town Tacos
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Nashville on the Southside of downtown in an area known as pie town. I guess it's because the neighborhood shaped like a pie. But anyhow here to check out a joint where a dude had a catering company and well pandemic hits loses 90% of his business But he adapts and overcomes as we do in the restaurant business and opens up a taco joint to keep everybody working. This is pie town tacos.

dishes.description: Tortilla chips topped with house-made queso, fried chicken dredged in a special breader, and a hot chicken spice blend bloomed in oil. Garnished with chopped cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Those are good chips. Great texture. Amazing crunch. The cotija's perfect. Good cheese sauce. I can't tell with my mouth fast enough, by the way. When you tell people in Nashville that they're getting Nashville hot chicken nachos, you got to deliver it. Legit, my friend.

restaurants.name: Centerpoint Barbecue
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm in the great state of Tennessee, one of my favorites. Why? Well, home of Jack Daniels, the Grand Ole Opry, and some killer barbecue. Now, I'm in Hendersonville, about 10 miles from Nashville, on the corner of Centerpoint and Main Street, to check out a joint that's got a ton of character and a bunch of stories. This is Centerpoint Barbecue.

dishes.description: Entire pork shoulder, bone-in, marinated in Malson and vinegar, rubbed with crazy pepper and salt. Smoked with shag bark hickory at 225 degrees for 18 hours, fat side up. Served with pickles and a cornbread patty.
dishes.guy_response: I got to tell you something. The pork has been cooked down to a point that you don't see very often. A little heat, a little sweet, right on your meat. Awesome job. That's what I think barbecue should taste like.

restaurants.name: The Grilled Cheeser
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Nashville, Tennessee. You got to love it. I mean, killer barbecue, great music, and you never know who you'll run into. So anyhow, I'm here to check out a joint that's doing grilled cheese, but grilled cheese to a different level. It's not just what they're serving. It's what they're serving it from. Get this, a food truck. This... What's that? Nah. Anyhow, this is the grilled cheeser.

dishes.description: Grilled cheese sandwich featuring Tennessee cheddar, house-made mayo, macaroni, roasted peppers, local tomatoes, and bacon bits. Built on bread buttered with a special mix of butter and mayo. Served with house-made pickles and roasted red pepper and tomato soup.
dishes.guy_response: So I gotta try this sandwich by itself. What do you think about that? You know what? I'll tell you something. If you're left with just matte pimento by itself, there's not enough texture. Yeah. Texture was the most. But you put the pasta in there, and the tomato sauce, and the tomato sauce, and the tomato sauce, and the tomato sauce, and the tomato sauce, and the tomato sauce, and the tomato sauce, and the tomato sauce, and the tomato sauce, and the tomato sauce, and the tomato sauce, but you put the pasta in there, and the tomato. A little bit of spice coming out of that, as well. This is where it goes on. Uh-huh. That's how you do it. This is so good. I'm gonna go into like a whole dream sequence. I am telling you what, this just takes me back to childhood.

dishes.description: Baked chicken (seasoned with margarine, salt, pepper, chili powder, brown sugar, paprika) shredded and added to a gravy made with margarine, celery, onion, garlic, chicken drippings, water, spices, and chicken base. Dumplings made from all-purpose and self-rising flour, salt, lard, and ice water, cooked in the fortified stock.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. That's about as old school as you're going to find them. All right. Some of that roasted chicken in there, mm. I'm just stalling so I can shovel it all in. It's a nice day. Day out. These are good spoons. Creaminess, tender chunks of chicken. I almost finished the whole bowl in that time. Good stuff.
